description_long: This object is a butterfly knife, also known as a balisong, featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a dark metallic finish. The blade is flanked by two pivoting handles, each with geometric cutouts and a matte blue-gray coloration, likely for weight reduction and grip. The handles appear to be constructed from metal, possibly steel or aluminum, with brass or bronze accents near the pivots. A latch mechanism is visible at the base, used to secure the handles together when closed. The overall design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation, emphasizing function and durability. The knife's compact, foldable form makes it suitable for urban utility or self-defense scenarios.
description: A butterfly knife with a straight, double-edged blade and blue-gray metal handles featuring geometric cutouts and brass accents. The design is functional and foldable, with a visible latch mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open blade and both handles with cutouts and brass accents.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the blade and handles, with the latch mechanism barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, highlighting the blade and handle symmetry.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and alignment of the handles.
attribute_handle_design: split, metal, geometric cutouts, matte finish, brass accents
